danger_signals.visual_form: Ghosts, mutants, monsters, bats, rabbits rendered as sprite enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Hazards that damage Gomez; require avoidance or stomping to eliminate

danger_signals.visual_form: Stars, swinging clock pendulums, fire lakes rendered as stage obstacles
danger_signals.meaning: Instant-damage zones that inflict harm regardless of enemy interaction

danger_signals.visual_form: Large sprite-scaled enemies holding Addams family members (Judge as final boss, Old Tree Boss, huge bird in garden)
danger_signals.meaning: Mandatory defeats required to rescue captive family members; gated progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Family members trapped in themed locations (Wednesday in ice area, Granny in stove, Morticia in sealed underground chambers)
danger_signals.meaning: World-state indicator of danger; establishes rescue motivation and level objectives

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items: sword, golf club, fezi-copter hat, speed shoes rendered as pickup objects
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary combat or mobility enhancement; reduces danger from enemies/hazards

safety_signals.visual_form: Heart containers (up to 5 max) and money ($25 per container fill) collectibles
safety_signals.meaning: Damage mitigation system; signals player survivability status

safety_signals.visual_form: 1-UP items scattered throughout mansion, particularly in Pugsley's Den (5 visible, 27 in secret area)
safety_signals.meaning: Death reprieve; indicates safe resource locations when discovered

safety_signals.visual_form: Interactive dialogue boxes provided by Thing character
safety_signals.meaning: Wayfinding aid; signals puzzle-solving guidance and directional safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Invisible door near front entrance leading to Pugsley's Den
safety_signals.meaning: Hidden safe area with abundant resources; requires knowledge to access

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Addams mansion interior aesthetic; character sprites of Gomez, Morticia, Pugsley, Wednesday, Granny, Uncle Fester, Lurch
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Gothic mansion setting; themed rooms (Music Room, Games Room, Portrait Gallery, Kitchen, outdoor garden); ice-themed freezing area (Wednesday's location); stove chamber (Granny's location); underground chambers (Morticia's location)

faction_indicators.faction: Antagonists (Abigail Craven, The Judge, Tully Alford)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: The Judge rendered as final boss sprite; Tully Alford controls mansion (represented through locked doors and passages); Abigail Craven's brainwashing of Uncle Fester (narrative state rather than visual marker)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ghosts, mutants, monsters serving as Abigail's minions infesting the mansion
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Locked chambers; puzzle-gated rooms (doors requiring keys in Amstrad CPC version); environmental hazards (stars, pendulums, fire lakes) as manifestations of hostile control; mansion corruption by infiltrating enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wednesday trapped in ice-themed room; Granny trapped in stove; Morticia sealed in underground chambers; Pugsley, Morticia, Wednesday held hostage in themed boss locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gomez must navigate and complete location-specific puzzles (deactivate stove, freeze-area navigation, activate underground chambers via Music Room tune); bosses act as captivity enforcers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Infestation of ghosts, mutants, monsters, bats, rabbits throughout all areas; hazard placement (swinging pendulums, fire lakes, stars); locked doors and sealed passages; corrupted safe spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Free enemy movement and spawn patterns; hazards remain active regardless of player interaction; environmental objects trigger damage on contact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Family members released from themed locations appear in Music Room; underground chambers accessible only after prerequisite rescues (Wednesday, Pugsley, Granny, Uncle Fester must meet Lurch to activate Morticia's chamber)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gomez gains access to previously locked areas; puzzle-gating removes; final Judge boss becomes accessible; narrative advancement signaled through location access changes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pugsley's Den (invisible door discovery) contains abundant 1-UPs (5 visible, 27 in secret subsection) and money; non-secret location bonus spots scattered throughout mansion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players can return to safe zones for resource farming; invisible door discovery represents meta-knowledge of safety infrastructure

environmental_storytelling.technique: Room thematic correlation to trapped character
environmental_storytelling.example: Wednesday located in ice-themed freezing area; Granny trapped in stove; Morticia sealed underground—each rescue zone visually communicates the captor's method of containment

Hall of Stairs as hub with six doorways leading to distinct 40-50 screen areas (kitchen, games room, portrait gallery, etc.); Zelda/Metroid-inspired open exploration allows player to discover narrative sequence through environmental discovery rather than forced linearity
Stars, swinging clock pendulums, fire lakes distributed throughout areas; visual manifestation of Abigail Craven's hostile control and The Judge's dominion over the mansion
Stove switch that must be found to free Granny; Music Room tune activation required for underground chamber access—environmental interaction directly tied to story progression and family rescue
Pugsley's Den (invisible door) and secret subsection (27 lives) signal safe family spaces untouched by antagonist corruption; thematic connection between Pugsley's presence and hidden abundance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Runs, jumps, squats throughout mansion; can stomp enemies; collects power-ups and coins; navigates freely between unlocked areas
Sword grants melee combat; golf club shoots projectiles; fezi-copter enables flight (disintegrates upon door transition); speed shoes increase movement velocity
Health represented by heart containers (2-5 max); $25 collected restores one container; invulnerability frames on hit (game boy version: Frankie potion grants 10-hit invulnerability)

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Enemy sprites (ghosts, mutants, monsters, bats, rabbits)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrol and spawn throughout infested areas; require stomping or power-up interaction to eliminate; deal damage on contact

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Boss entities (The Judge, Old Tree Boss, huge garden bird)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationed in family member containment areas; must be defeated to free hostages; represent final obstacle before area progression
Plays specific tune in Music Room to activate underground chambers; presence indicates prerequisite completion (Wednesday, Pugsley, Granny, Uncle Fester must be rescued first)
Offers clues in hint boxes; explains room mechanics and directional guidance; scattered throughout mansion as wayfinding aid
Remain active without player interaction; inflict damage on contact regardless of combat state; cannot be eliminated; signal hazard zones through placement pattern

Remain stationary in themed containment areas (Wednesday frozen, Granny in stove, Morticia underground); release upon boss defeat or puzzle completion; convergence in Music Room signals narrative progression

notes: The Addams Family (SNES 1992) uses thematic environmental design to communicate world-state. Danger is signaled through infestation (enemy types), hazard visibility (environmental obstacles), and captivity markers (themed imprisonment locations). Safety is indicated through resource clustering (hidden rooms, power-up concentration), puzzle completion (unlock progression), and NPC presence (guidance from Thing/Lurch). Faction identity relies on character sprites and mansion architecture rather than color-coding or symbolic sigils—the Addams family identity is communicated through the Gothic setting itself, while antagonist presence manifests through environmental corruption (enemies, hazards, locked passages). The game's non-linear structure (Zelda/Metroid-inspired) allows players to discover narrative through spatial exploration; family member locations serve as both story beats and area objectives. Political situation (hostile takeover by Abigail/Judge/Tully) is expressed through loss of access (locked doors, sealed areas) and environmental infestation rather than dialogue or cutscenes.
